scenes.captionText: Long, long ago, before anything else, there was God. God made everything: the sky, the land, and the deep blue water.

scenes.altText: Adam and Eve in the garden
scenes.captionText: God made the first people, Adam and Eve. He loved them very much, gave them a beautiful garden, and told them to care for it.

scenes.altText: People building the Tower of Babel
scenes.captionText: Many years passed, and there were lots of people in the world. Instead of filling the whole earth like God asked, they decided to stay in one place and build a very tall tower.
God saw their pride and knew this wasn't what was best for them. So, He did something amazing: He mixed up their words so they couldn't understand each other!
This place was called Babel. Because they couldn't talk to each other, the families had to stop building. They packed up and finally started to move away to find new homes all over the earth.
This great scattering of families is called the 'Table of Nations'. It tells us where different groups of people traveled to start their new lives.
One important family leader was named Mizraim. He traveled far away and settled his family along a huge, beautiful river in Africa.
The land where Mizraim settled became very famous. Today, we call this place Egypt! They built grand cities and pyramids there.
Another family leader was named Cush. He also moved his family to Africa, but he went a little further south than Mizraim.
The place where Cush and his family settled is called Nubia. It was a beautiful land filled with strong warriors and rich treasures.
A third leader was named Phut. He took his family to live in a wide, sunny place to the west of Egypt, a place called Libya.
Wow, what a journey! God loved all these families in their new homes. Now, let's see what you remember. Can you remember which son went to Egypt?
That's right, it was Mizraim! He traveled down the river to start his family's new home in Egypt.
Great job! And who was the leader that settled in the land of Nubia, south of Egypt?
Yes, it was Cush! These leaders helped start some of the most amazing kingdoms in Africa.
